methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $43M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Drones & Autonomous (found 1 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 4 same-sector peers exist in Drones & Autonomous, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Tevel Aerobotics, founded by Yaniv Maor, is a Series B-stage autonomous systems company operating in the Drones & Autonomous sector. The company has raised $42.6M to date. The valuation universe comprises seven private peers in the sector, including Elroy Air, Joby Aviation, Nuro, Archer Aviation, and Manna Aero; no public comparables exist in this narrow segment.

The analysis applies one applicable method given data constraints. Stage-progression triangulation, which uses the $42.6M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields an implied valuation of $81M. The peer-median method is not applicable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comps; while four same-sector peers exist, none of the narrow filters (same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier) produced the minimum three datapoints required for meaningful statistical inference.

The triangulation supports a valuation range of $69M (low) to $93M (high), with a median of $81M. This range reflects the single applicable method and carries material uncertainty owing to the sparse peer dataset and reliance on stage-progression heuristics rather than market-based multiples. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation within the autonomous systems market, revenue trajectory post-Series B, and customer concentration risk, none of which are quantified in available data.
